title: strict-priority + FIFO pick_next (scheduler)
statement: From docs/basket/spec-scheduler.md §1.4/§3.1-3.2 and vectors T5/T6. Implement the priority run-queues: `NPRIO = 4` strict levels 0 (highest) .. 3 (lowest); within a level, FIFO order. Define `struct Sched { q: [4][8]u32, count: [4]u32 }` (each level a FIFO of up to 8 task ids, `count[p]` its length). Define `fn admit(s: write Sched, task: u32, prio: u32) -> bool` — append `task` at the TAIL of level `prio`, returning `false` if `prio >= 4` or the level is full, else `true`. Define `fn pick_next(s: write Sched) -> i64` — remove and return (as `i64`) the HEAD task of the lowest-numbered non-empty level (strict priority, then FIFO), or `-1` if all levels are empty. Write-path derefs are explicit (`s.*.count[p]`). Define only these items.
